channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Florida education bill sparks debate over funds for advanced placement programs
item.description: Florida lawmakers battled it out on the House floor this week over a bill that could lead to significant funding cuts to education.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he would respect Supreme Court decision to return wrongly deported man
item.description: President Trump said Friday he would respect a Supreme Court decision to bring back a Maryland man the administration mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump feels in ‘good shape,’ after physical, says he got ‘every question right’ on cognitive test
item.description: President Trump talked to reporters on Friday about his first physical examination and cognitive test of his second term at Walter Reed Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration scores win as judge clears way for ICE enforcement in churches, places of worship
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that ICE agents can make arrests at churches and other places of worship, rejecting claims from religious groups of rights violations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California a ‘broken state': Governor hopeful Stephen Cloobeck
item.description: Cloobeck's ideas to turn things around include revamping California’s entertainment tax credit program.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson (R-East Grand Forks) said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about the Social Security Administration listing thousands of living immigrants as dead
item.description: The Trump administration’s move to classify thousands of living immigrants as dead and cancel their Social Security numbers is an escalation of the president’s crackdown on people who were legally allowed to live in the U.S. under programs instituted by his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump opens window for a deal with Iran but issues warning if things don't work out
item.description: President Donald Trump is betting that a beleaguered Iran is so vulnerable following a tumultuous 18 months in the Middle East that it might finally be ready to abandon its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal judge hammers DOJ on whereabouts of alleged MS-13 gang member following SCOTUS order
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland sharply questioned DOJ attorneys over the location of an alleged MS-13 gang member who was deported to El Salvador last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP ethics complaint alleges conflict by DFL Senate President Bobby Joe Champion
item.description: Six GOP lawmakers said the Minneapolis Democrat broke Senate rules when he carried legislation funding the nonprofit of a former legal client.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Iowa Republican Gov. Kim Reynolds announces she won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: In a surprise announcement Friday, Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds said she will not seek a third term in office in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pentagon fires Greenland US base commander who 'undermined' JD Vance after Pituffik visit
item.description: Col. Susan Meyers, commander of the U.S. Space Force base in Greenland, was relieved of duty after sending an email in which she distanced herself from Vice President JD Vance’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Republicans demand RFK Jr reverse Biden-era 'public health crisis' on guns
item.description: A group of House Republicans is writing to Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., asking him to crack down on gun control funding in his department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump nominee Caine for chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff in overnight vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff on Friday, filling the position almost two months after President Donald Trump fired his predecessor. Trump nominated Caine to become the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Head of US Space Force base in Greenland is fired after Vance visit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The commander of a U.S. Space Force base in Greenland has been fired after she sent a base-wide email breaking with official messaging following Vice President JD Vance’s visit to the Danish territory that President Donald Trump is seeking to annex.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security lists thousands of living immigrants as dead to prompt them to leave, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has moved to classify more than 6,000 living immigrants as dead, canceling their Social Security numbers and effectively wiping out their ability to work or receive benefits in an effort to get them to leave the country, according to two people familiar wit
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic prosecutor in crime-torn Albuquerque launches bid for New Mexico governor
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A Democratic district attorney in the eye of New Mexico’s tempest over crime and accountability launched his candidacy for governor Thursday as the National Guard prepares to deploy to the state’s largest city to shore up public safety.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health workers rally against Trump administration cuts
item.description: Members of the Minnesota Association of Professional Employees rallied outside a state office building on the grounds of the State Capitol Thursday to speak out against cuts to public health programs.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Giant bust of Elon Musk's head vandalized near Starbase in Texas
item.description: "We don't want this to happen again because it was an effort to put it up and very expensive," the owner said.
item.dc:creator: Mia Morales, NewsNation

item.title: New York lawmakers push 'Peanut's Law' to reform wildlife enforcement
item.description: The death of P'Nut, an Instagram-famous squirrel, inspired legislation aimed at reforming wildlife enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump tariff drama
item.description: Financial markets soared on Wednesday after President Trump issued a 90-day pause in almost all the tariffs that had roiled the global economy during the past week.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: House passes bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in a move that would vastly diminish the ability of courts to block President Trump’s policies. Dubbed the No Rogue Rulings Act, the legislation from Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: U.S. stocks soar after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks surged on Wednesday after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on the higher tariffs he had imposed on most countries — except for China.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam, MPR News

item.title: Why Trump faces toughest House GOP test yet on budget blueprint
item.description: Wednesday’s looming House vote on the Senate’s framework for advancing Trump’s legislative agenda is shaping up to be one of the president’s toughest tests yet on Capitol Hill, as hardline Republicans dismiss the White House’s public — and private — entreaties to line up behind the measure that will
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FEMA to revoke $188M in New York City migrant grants
item.description: The money is part of the Shelter and Services Program, which helps local governments provide for migrants.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: As cost of new products rises, many thrifty shoppers look to secondhand
item.description: President Trump has said tariffs will counter imbalance in the country’s trade partnerships, but experts have warned this could lead to higher costs for American businesses and consumers — which could mean more people shop secondhand.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders White House to allow AP access to news events
item.description: U.S. Judge Trevor N. McFadden rules the White House cannot deny the Associated Press access to news events because the wire service continues to use “Gulf of Mexico” rather than “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News
